schema_notes: Anchor facts only. JSON shape, field names, and filenames are the executor's choice — the grader checks semantic content, not key naming. Anchored to vercel/next.js v15.3.0 (2025-04-09) AND v15.3.1 (2025-04-17) plus 5 immutable YouTube video IDs.

version_diff_facts.characterisation: v15.3.1 is a small patch release; release notes are dramatically shorter than the v15.3.0 feature release notes.

videos.title: Next.js in 100 Seconds // Plus Full Beginner's Tutorial
Next js Tutorial for Beginners | Nextjs 13 (App Router) with TypeScript
Next.js 15 Crash Course | Build and Deploy a Production-Ready Full Stack App

subtitle_keyword_analysis.method_hint: yt-dlp --write-auto-subs --skip-download --sub-lang en for each video ID; case-insensitive substring count of each keyword in the resulting .en.vtt file. Absolute counts drift slightly across yt-dlp player versions and Google ASR refreshes, so the grader checks the rank ordering and the strict winner rather than exact numbers.
subtitle_keyword_analysis.best_coverage_rationale: Zq5fmkH0T78 covers 6 of the 7 v15 keywords (only 'router cache' missing as a 2-word phrase) and totals 482 occurrences across keywords — the next-best video (mTz0GXj8NN0) only covers 3 keywords with 106 total occurrences. The 6-vs-3 coverage gap and ~4.5x occurrence gap make Zq5fmkH0T78 the strict winner under any reasonable scoring of either coverage breadth or absolute frequency.
subtitle_keyword_analysis.tolerance: Per-keyword raw counts may drift ±30% between yt-dlp/Google-ASR refreshes; the ordinal ranking (Zq5fmkH0T78 strictly highest on both topics_covered AND total_occurrences) is the stable property. The grader does not require exact counts.

expected_top_recommendation.rationale: Only v15-era video and covers ~6/7 of the v15-specific topics; should be ranked first / recommended as the starting point for any learner targeting v15.3.1.
